raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request asks for judgment on the paper idea, making the thesis, gap, and contribution framing centrally relevant.","R1.2":"Assessing what one thinks of the artifact centrally involves checking whether the described method, motivation, and conclusion cohere logically.","R1.3":"The artifact makes empirical claims, so judging whether the reported results support those claims is a central responsibility.","R1.4":"The introduction positions ToolASR relative to recent tool-use and end-to-end ASR work, making novelty assessment central to the requested opinion.","RX.1":"Any projection of an open-ended opinion request should disclose uncertainty about unstated evaluation criteria and missing paper context.","RX.2":"The response should avoid overstating what can be concluded from the excerpt alone.","RX.3":"The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation, not executing a review or rewriting the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid encouraging unsupported research claims or misleading evaluation practices downstream.","RX.5":"The rationale should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's explicit claims."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request asks for judgment on the paper idea, making the thesis, gap, and contribution framing centrally relevant.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing what one thinks of the artifact centrally involves checking whether the described method, motivation, and conclusion cohere logically.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical claims, so judging whether the reported results support those claims is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: The introduction positions ToolASR relative to recent tool-use and end-to-end ASR work, making novelty assessment central to the requested opinion.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended opinion request should disclose uncertainty about unstated evaluation criteria and missing paper context.
rationale.RX.2: The response should avoid overstating what can be concluded from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation, not executing a review or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid encouraging unsupported research claims or misleading evaluation practices downstream.
rationale.RX.5: The rationale should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's explicit claims.
